title: scandix pecten-veneris subsp. hispanica
intro: Scandix pecten-veneris subsp. hispanica, also known as Shepherd's Needle or Scandix, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Scandix pecten-veneris subsp. hispanica include its ability to relieve urinary tract issues, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, such as kidney stones and urinary infections.
